metadata.title: NM COA Figure A - North Region Plugging Book

generalProcedures.text: The OCD district office must be given a minimum of 24 hours notice prior to setting any plugs. Contact the Santa Fe OCD office at (505) 476-3440.

The operator of a well must have a representative on location during the setting of all plugs. The operator's representative cannot be an employee of the plugging contractor unless the contractor and the operator are the same entity.
All cement plugs shall be placed by circulation or squeeze method through tubing or drill pipe. Casing alone may not be used as a conduit for cement.

All cement for plugging shall be conventional hard-setting cement mixed in accordance with API standards. Class C cement shall be used above 6,500 ft; Class H cement shall be used at or below 6,500 ft. Slurry weight: Class C = 14.8 lbs/gal, Class H = 16.4 lbs/gal.
Minimum plug length shall be 100 feet OR 25 sacks of cement, whichever is greater.
All cement plugs shall have sufficient slurry volume to fill the calculated height plus cement excess: 50% for cased hole, 100% for open hole.
Maximum spacing between plugs shall not exceed 3,000 feet in cased hole or 2,000 feet in open hole.
All cement plugs shall be tagged and tested to verify placement. Plugs across usable quality water, surface casing shoe, and productive formations must be tagged.
A cast iron bridge plug (CIBP) used as a plug base shall have a minimum of 100 feet of cement on top of the plug. If set with a bailer, 35 feet of cement on top is acceptable.
A cement plug shall extend at least 50 feet above and 50 feet below each zone requiring isolation (usable quality water base, surface casing shoe, productive horizons).
Cement shall stand for a minimum wait-on-cement (WOC) time of 4 hours before disturbing. In this region, a minimum of 8 hours WOC is recommended before testing or drilling out cement.

Formation isolation is mandatory for every NM well. All productive horizons and usable quality water zones must be isolated by separate cement plugs. No plug may span across non-isolated intervals greater than 3,000 feet (cased) or 2,000 feet (open hole).

township_range_boundaries.note: Figure A covers all NM counties NOT included in Figure B (south Artesia/Chaves south), Figure C (potash enclave Eddy/Lea), or Figure D (Hobbs district Lea/Eddy east). Default region for any NM well not matching other COA figures.
exclusions: Chaves (south portions per Figure B boundaries)
Eddy (north/west portions per Figure B; potash enclave per Figure C; east portions per Figure D)
Lea (potash enclave per Figure C; Hobbs district per Figure D)

subAreas.name: San Juan Basin
subAreas.description: Northwestern NM, San Juan County and surrounding area. Primary coal-bed methane and conventional gas producing region. Shallow gas formations with water zone protection critical.

plugSpecification.notes: Coal-bed methane zone; must isolate from shallower water zones
Isolate if productive or if usable water present

subAreas.name: Northwest Shelf / Four Corners
subAreas.description: Transition zone between San Juan Basin and other NM basins. Includes carbonate and clastic formations with oil production from San Andres and Yeso.

plugSpecification.notes: Below 6500 ft use Class H cement

subAreas.name: Central / Eastern NM Basin
subAreas.description: Includes Raton Basin, Tucumcari Basin, and transitional areas of central and eastern NM. Shallower oil wells with Permian and Pennsylvanian targets.

casing.description: NM cement sack requirements for cased hole - 50% excess factor (flat, not depth-based)

openHole.description: NM cement sack requirements for open hole - 100% excess factor (flat, not depth-based)

woc_time.description: Minimum 4 hours wait-on-cement before any disturbance. Recommend 8 hours or until 500 psi compressive strength achieved.

max_plug_spacing.description: Plugs must be set at intervals not exceeding 3000 ft (cased) or 2000 ft (open hole) per NMAC 19.15.25.

formation_isolation.description: Formation isolation is mandatory for every NM well. All productive horizons and usable quality water zones must be isolated.

surface_casing.description: Surface casing may not be removed from a well at abandonment per NMAC 19.15.25.
